My guess is, more people were in on this elaborate scam.

title: Simplifying Radicals - Civil Service Exam Review #mathteachergon #cse2023
body:
√40
9 * 5 - Find the factors that contains a perfect square (In this case, 9)
√9 * √5 - Square root both sides
3 * √5 - if not factors, let it be
= 3√5

title: DLSU TOUR!📚🤪realistic week in dlsu, uni day in the life | Reese Kaw
body:
De La Salle had an vintage school battle song that describes the pre-war school campus as being near the sea. The song tells about pre-war Lasallites then enjoying the fresh cool sea breeze coming from Manila Bay from their classrooms and campus dormitories.
Our iconic Saint La Salle building back then was the tallest structure from Taft Avenue to Roxas Blvd. The students who resided in campus were on the top fourth floor and they would often the describe the luxury of witnessng Manila's coastal boulevard daily sunrise and sunset. It was only USAFFE General McArthur that would have had the same privilege to see this natural spectacle from his luxury top penthouse of the top of his pre-war Manila Hotel beside Luneta park.
author: @deangomez9268

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body:
For first time viewers, here are the timestamps


What is the difference between Ab and BS Psyche - 0:30
How many years is the course? - 0:51
Is Psychology hard? - 1:06
Is it a pre-med course? - 1:20
What are the career opportunities - 1:37
What is the difference between Psychologist and Psychiatrist? - 2:09
Is AB Psych a pre-law? - 2:39
What are the specialized subj? - 2:51
Is there any math? - 3:20
Any tips for incoming freshmen students? - 3:45
Can we take Psychology is we studied HUMMS in Senior HS? - 4:15
Can you read minds? - 4:53
Are there lab subjs? - 5:01
Do you learn how to interpret dreams? - 5:12
Does the course make you cry? – 5:40
Does it really make you go crazy? – 6:00
What is the hardest subj so far? – 6:24
Do you naturally read a person’s behavior after learning about it in Psychology? – 7:08
Rate the course from 1 to 10? – 7:45
Why do so many people belittle Psychology? – 8:26
author: @KathleenKateMuzic

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body:
I'm a Psych grad as well and currently furthering my studies in the MAs (Road to RPsy woot woot!) and I agree with the stigma thingy because here in PH a lot of people would judge you when they see you visiting a Psychiatrist/Psychologist. They be like seeking help from these professionals would equate to you being "baliw" already. I have heard a lot of people talking shit about Psych and discriminating people who suffer from mental illnesses. It's sad but we need to see this as a challenge to spread more awareness as much as we can.
Good luck with the OJT girls!
author: @maryannl767

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: Psychology graduate na and gonna take the psychometrician board exam this year hehe. And I'm happy to see na marami na ang may interest sa psychology and this video helps clear out some misconceptions 😊
author: @kayeapril1148

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: I'm planning to take BS Psychology even though mahina ako sa math and memorization feel ko maiiyak na ko sa mga nalaman ko goshhhh but i know i can improve myself if i work harddd so tiwala langg!!
author: @LX8584

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: I took Bs Psychology before pero nag stop ako kase sobrang hirap and nahirapan din ako i manage yung time ko dahil nag wowork ako.  Pero after ma diagnosed ng 2 sisters ko ng anxiety and depression i decided na i pursue yung course ko. Yes sobrang hirap pero ganon talaga wala naman madaling course 🤷🏻‍♂️ .  And lalo na dito sa Philippines tumataas yung bilang ng mga taong nagkaka anxiety, depression and suicide..  Actually kaya nahihirapan mag open up yung iba is because dahil sa STIGMA and hopefully maging mas open tayo when it comes to this matter..
author: @aidencohen2501

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: I keep on watching Vlogs about psychology because I'm already decided to shift to BS Psychology from Engineering. I really want to be in a helping profession for I believe that my passion is to serve people. Thank you for this Vlog!!!
author: @markjosephpm4226

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body:
As a mental health professional and psychology professor, it is with great joy that I see a lot of young people interested in our field :) Thank you for this informative video po :)

For those who are considering psychology as their course, I do hope you would push through if possible. Our country needs a lot of psychology professionals :)
author: @jaxdenielepagtalunan4148

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: It's nice that you do these things as you know we battle the stigma given to Psychology and mental health everyday and by doing these, people get to learn a thing or two hence they get a clearer mindset of the issue. I hope one day I could have a conversation with you about psychology and we could learn from each other. Perhaps, in one of KKD's game? 😂 anyway, you've got a long way ahead of you in the field. Just be persistent and committed to your goal and you'll get there. Always give your full attention and listen very well to your professors and understand all the psychology concepts because soon enough you'll be a torch bearer and guiding light for people hiding in the dark because of the fear of being judged because they have a mental health condition. Trust me, it'll be the most fulfilling thing ever!!! ❤ good luck on your studies, psychmate!!
author: @marcjoffet

title: Q&A: DLSU Psychology (Philippines)
body: ghad this is my dream course!! I want to pursue it but my parents don't want me to take up this course. :'< Ahhhhh, observing people and understanding their behaviours are the things i love to study! But unfortunately, my parents are one of those who misinterpret what this course actually is. So sad. Next year, i'll be in college. Hayst, dont want to be undecided until this SY ends.

title: DLSU Manila Campus Tour! *Christmas Edition*
body: Not sure if your question was already answered (couldn’t really see all comments-only the top and newest), but the oldest building there is the LS building.  Now I am not sure which came next, either SJ (which might be because it is closer to LS) or Benilde (this is the building you guys call Miguel). The name of the Benilde building was changed to Miguel when CSB was established. The Velasco building was only built on the old football field, in 1980.  Thanks for the tour.  The campus has really changed a lot!
author: @peterdasilva8850

De La Salle had an vintage school battle song that describes the pre-war school campus as being near the sea. The song tells about pre-war Lasallites then enjoying the fresh cool sea breeze coming from Manila Bay from their classrooms and campus dormitories.
Our iconic Saint La Salle building back then was the tallest structure from Taft Avenue to Roxas Blvd. The students who resided in campus were on the top fourth floor and they would often the describe the luxury of witnessng Manila's coastal boulevard daily sunrise and sunset. title: Day in the Life in DLSU the Top Clout-Chasing School of the Philippines #dlsu #lasalle
body:
SIGNUM FIDEI - Saint La Salle's Way

GOD is our Light as we pray
"Live JESUS, In Our Hearts, Forever!
Saint La Salle is our Spark as we pray "I will continue O my GOD to do all my actions for the Love of You."
Lasallians become beacons of HOPE for others inspired by our school motto of Live by the Spirit of FAITH through our actions, Zeal for SERVICE, and COMMUNION in Mission as Lasallians go to school as they "Enter to LEARN" and "Leave to SERVE" to be a "Sign of FAITH" for others.
To be "SIGNUMFIDEI" is our way.
It is Saint La Salle's way.
author: @deangomez9268

title: Day in the Life in DLSU the Top Clout-Chasing School of the Philippines #dlsu #lasalle
body:
Just a few, of the Filipino Lasallian Alumni Billionaires, Business Taipans educated and cared for several years during their early elementary, high school and college educational years by their Christian Brothers classroom mentors of De La Salle...
1. Don Paquito Ortigas - Generously donated the post-war La Salle Green Hills, Ortigas Campus
2. Don Enrique Zobel together with his son Santiago Zobel - Generously donated 2 spacious Lasallian Campuses in their Ayala, Alabang
3. Don Enrique Razon Sr. of ICTSI - Donated the Green Archer Sports Gym of DLSU
4. Don Ricky Razon Jr. of ICTSI - Generously provided the ultra-modern, multi-level Logistical Building at the very new DLSU-STC, Laguna Campus and is the present chief financial manager of the multi-champion De La Salle Green Archers.
5. Bilyonaryo Magazine's - Frederico "Piki" Lopez, an old classmate and friend of fellow Lasallian and business partner Filipino Billionaire Ricky Razon of ICTSI
6. Ambassador Alphonso Yuchengco of RCBC - Generously provided the multi-level Yuchengco Bldg and the beautiful multi-level Theater
7. Ambassador Danding Cojuangco of SMC - generously gave thousands of Lasallian academic scholarships for faculty MBA and PhD development. The generous Ambassador was the longest 30-year-long Financial Team Manager of the former DLSC in the NCAA and modern UAAP champion DLSU Green Archer teams. The very generous Lasallian die-hard has also generously donated several campus developments for the Lasallian campuses of DLSU-Dasmarinas and USLS-Bacolod.
8. Ms. Claudia Diaz Cojuangco - The current very supportive and generous financial team manager of the multi-UAAP champion Lady Spiker Team of DLSU.
9. RFM, Selecta and Go Negosyo - CEO Joey Concepcion former DLSU Green Archer Financial Team Manager
10. CEO of Concepcion Industries, Motorola, Mariwasa and Namfrel Chairman Joe-Con Concepcion
11. Ambassador and CEO Jorge Araneta of Araneta Cubao Commercial Center - provided the 2 beautiful huge campuses of DLSAU.
12. Billionaire Architect Torralba - generously provided the very scenic hilltop Lasallian campus beautifully located on top of Antipolo.
13. Billionaire National Architect - Archt. Leandro Locsin - generously provided the whole 50+hectare DLSU-STC Laguna Campus with additional hectares for future development and expansion.
14. CEO George Yang of McDonald's Philippines
15. Senator Vicente Ting Paterno - CEO of 7-11 Philippines
16.Secretary Jose Pardo - CEO of Wendy's Hamburger Philippines
17.CEO Leo Prieto of Dunkin Donuts-Philippines
18. Billionaire Gonzalo Co It of Green Cross Rubbing Alcohol and Zonrox
19. SM Billionaire Henry Sy Sr. - Generously provided DLSU's multi-level modern library building (AFSC)
20. SM Billionaire Harley Sy - Lasallian Engineer
21. SM Billionaire Hilbert Sy - Lasallian Engineer
22. SM Billionaire Hans Sy - Lasallian Engineer
23. SM Billionaire Henry Sy Jr. - Lasallian Engineer
24.CEO John Gokongwei of Robinson's Malls - generously donated 2 Computer Engineering Buildings one along Taft Avenue and the other is the new Ubisoft Game Department Bldg at the 50 hectare DLSU-STC, Laguna Campus.
25. Rufino Family - Generously provided DLSU's modern multi-level Tanada-Diokno Law campus area and it's multi-level building at BGC.
26. Villavicencio Family - has always constantly provided several of DLSU's campus developments
27. Lhullier Family - generously provided for DLSC's campus development after WWII.
28. Aboitiz Family - provided for the post-war development of the severely damaged DLSC campus and its very beautiful school chapel.
29.Litton Family - generously donated for the post-war reconstruction of the heavily damaged De La Salle College, Taft
30. Don Soriano of the old SMC - provided for the campus development and reconstruction of post-war De La Salle College
31. Edgar Saavedra
32. Marie Grace Uy
33. Dennis Uy
34. Michael Consiquien
35. All strongly united together in their traditional spirit of INDIVISA MANENT deep sense of Lasallian brotherhood, togetherness and family with several other fellow die-hard, very supportive multi-millionaire and billionaire Lasallian alumni from the Lasallian campuses of LSGH-Ortigas, DLSZ-Ayala Alabang, USLS Bacolod all of them eagerly wanting to generously provide for the further development and expansion of the educational Catholic mission of the Christian Brothers of Saint Jean-Baptiste de La Salle, FSC...
author: @deangomez9268

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: As a member of the poorer community always passing by La Salle wondering how the upper class  go about their schooling, I appreciate this vlog. Now I know how it looks from the inside lol
author: @happybanana0929

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: I noticed how vibrant and fun La Salle Taft is compared to Ateneo campus which is a little bit quiet and kind of that monastery vibe inside. Both are indeed elite school though. But La Salle offers more scholarships among poor and less fortunate ones.
author: @polingduran3392

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!

body: I’m taking my Master’s Degree at DLSU. Haven’t explored the entire campus, I’m jealous! I usually  have classes on evening and weekends. I love it there. Animo La Salle! 💚
author: @ChrisCaluag

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: You did it,  The Big Four of philippine universities... Plus a lot quality content here!!! God bless you; Kell you deserve an award! And thank you for loving the philippines :))
author: @33Haunted

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: Fun fact: My art teacher while I was studying in DLSU told us that the Henry Sy Library building's architecture was inspired by trees where people can find shade and lay down under it, hence the pillars. For few years since it was built, students can actually lay down by the pillars, just like being under a tree but recently the rules have changed and they put benches around the pillars.
author: @jerico3157

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: Ok you are indeed a master in making your way in. Now here's your real challenge. Can you tour us inside the Philippine Military Academy in Baguio? 😁
author: @pagioartor8871

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: Also a pure blooded Lasallian, I encourage you to visit De La Salle Medical and Health Sciences Institute and De La Salle Dasmarinas in Cavite. Those two universities are the best eco-friendly university in National Capital Region.  Enjoy and Animo La Salle!
author: @daddyBinoyRN

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: After seeing your posts on UST, UP and Ateneo, I began to wonder if you'd also visit my alma mater and you did! 👏 The Henry Sy building (the one standing on pillars) actually replaced La Salle's very own football field. It was sad to see the field go but then we have a really huge library to replace it. 😅
author: @jandjlifestyle

title: Foreigner Reacts to DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY (DLSU)! Filipino University Tour!
body: maybe next time sir,  you interview some students to know more about the universities like some trivias and other stuffs. i am sure it will be amazing.
author: @rmnavera4683

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: I love the way the De La Salle Christian Brothers turned back time by returning the original pre-war Saint La Salle Hall design of Tomas Mapua. I love looking at the iconic grand neo-classic stately structure preserved and immortalized for all architectural and design students to study and marvel at.
author: @deangomez9268

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: As a graduate of said school, andami nang nagbago diyan. Nakakamiss tumambay sa Agno, lalo na since the pandemic began.
author: @RalphDomingo1989

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body:
I've always wanted to be one of those beautiful De La Sallian people, the esteem of being associated with the status of the school. It's like having a seemingly better existence than the majority of your peers... but it didn't happen to me.

That's why i like watching university walk vlogs, it gives a tiny bit of what's it like be on the prestigious university i couldn't experience.
author: @Ianseraspe1219

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: I was in elementary school at DLSU back in the mid-Seventies. So odd to see how the area has changed… and how it has not.
author: @jossasson

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: 0:58 Sa LRT nakalagay VERTICAL CLEARANCE 14 FEET. Back in the days nung dekada 80s ang sukatan noon ay Imperial System pa (feet, inches, pounds, etc.) as opposed to the Metric System (meters, centimeters, kilos, etc.) na ginagamit na natin ngayon.
author: @florante4991

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: I miss benilde! I miss Taft! So much has changed. I haven't been there for almost a decade now. Good thing I found your channel lol
author: @q_2676

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: Graduated here 12 years ago, which feels just like yesterday. Damn, place looks a lot different now. I see that the Agno entrance nalipat na pala sa dulo
author: @justmytwocentsdontgetmad

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: Thanks for the video. Always go there during my early college days to watch and cheer for our school on NCAA basketball games. Best days during the San Beda-San Sebastian, LaSalle-Ateneo, Mapua-Letran rivalry.
author: @utubefanguyyy982

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: ive been a kulasa for a very long time and im now a frosh at dlsu! its still the same, but at the same time completely different
author: @taniesaz

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: Originally walang steel fence protecting our pre-war LS Bldg. All white stately looking facade with a huge green lawn. The old DLS campus had the biggest football field with lots of trees providing shade to pre-war Lasallites. The Taft Avenue area had huge trees that lined up the street. The area was a purely residential area of wealthy Manilenos. The Aboitiz, Ayala, Zobel, Ortigas,  Araneta, and Cojuangco families and all the well heeled families of Manila resided nearby pre-war De La Salle - Taft.
author: @deangomez9268

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: One thing I like taft along dlsu there are guards that would assist the crossing of their students. This is just one of the perks .
author: @priscillaasis8889

title: Walking Tour Around De La Salle University Area | Malate University Belt, Taft Manila | Philippines
body: Hay I miss that area! Im from CSB ID 105, dunno but first thing that i remebered was the baha and the overpriced charge of the pedicabs which was P30.00 sometimes P60.00 from CSB main to CSB Hotel 😂, this was way back 2005
author: @charlesvillegas6834

title: La Salle University| Quick Tour( Philly)
body: I'm considering going to La Salle atm so I just have a few questions. How would you describe the atmosphere there, like, are people friendly or competitive? Do most of your professors do their best to help students? Is the student body diverse or are most people in frats/sororities?
author: @746Nadine

title: DE LA SALLE UNIVERSITY - MANILA CAMPUS #school #dlsu #shorts #short #shortvideo #shortvideos
body:
Saint La Salle's Way of SIGNUM🌟FIDEI

GOD is our Light as we pray
"Live✝️JESUS In Our❤️Hearts, Forever! Amen."
Saint La🌟Salle is our spark as we pray "I will continue, O my GOD, to do all my actions for my Love of You."
Lasallians become Beacons of HOPE for others, inspired by our old school Lasallian traditional prayers of "Live by the Spirit of FAITH, Live by the Spirit Zeal through SERVICE, and Live by the Spirit of COMMUNION through Lasallian our mission. Our traditional Lasallian ethos of RELIGIO, MORES ET CULTURA will be our moral compass. All Lasallians are inspired to go to school as they all "Enter to LEARN, Leave to SERVE", to be a Sign of FAITH for others and make a difference to TEACH Minds, TOUCH Hearts and TRANSFORM the Lives of others.

To be SIGNUM🌟FIDEI is our way.
It is Saint La🌟Salle's way.
author: @deangomez9268
